source: An Act to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide for the deductibility of certain medical expenses for individuals over the age of 65. SECTION 1. This Act may be cited as the Senior Medical Expense Deduction Act. SECTION 2. The Internal Revenue Code is amended by adding a new section permitting taxpayers aged 65 and over to deduct qualified medical expenses without regard to the 7.5 percent adjusted gross income threshold. SECTION 3. This Act shall take effect for taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.
reference: Senior Medical Expense Deduction Act. Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers age 65 and older to deduct qualified medical expenses without the 7.5% AGI threshold. Effective for tax years after Dec 31, 2025.
candidate: Senior Medical Expense Deduction Act amends the Internal Revenue Code so taxpayers aged 65 and over can deduct qualified medical expenses without the 7.5 percent adjusted gross income floor. The change takes effect for taxable years beginning after December 31, 2025.

source: A Bill to establish a national strategy on energy storage research and development. SECTION 1. Short title: Energy Storage Research Act. SECTION 2. The Secretary of Energy shall establish a 10 year program for the research and development of long duration energy storage technologies, including but not limited to flow batteries, compressed air, thermal storage, and hydrogen. SECTION 3. Authorizes 2 billion dollars over 5 years for grants to universities, national laboratories, and private companies.
reference: Energy Storage Research Act. Directs the Secretary of Energy to launch a 10-year research and development program for long-duration storage technologies (flow batteries, compressed air, thermal, hydrogen) and authorizes $2B over 5 years in grants.
candidate: The Energy Storage Research Act directs the Secretary of Energy to set up a 10-year R&D program for long-duration storage (flow batteries, compressed air, thermal, hydrogen) and authorizes 2 billion dollars over 5 years in grants.

source: A Bill amending the Fair Labor Standards Act to require predictive scheduling for retail and food service workers. SECTION 1. Short title: Predictive Scheduling Act. SECTION 2. Employers with more than 50 employees shall provide written work schedules at least 14 days in advance. SECTION 3. Employees shall receive premium pay for shifts changed with less than 7 days notice. SECTION 4. Violations subject to civil penalties up to 1000 dollars per violation.
reference: Predictive Scheduling Act. Amends the FLSA to require employers with >50 workers to post schedules 14 days ahead, pay premiums for changes within 7 days, with $1,000 civil penalties.
candidate: This bill mandates that all retail and food service businesses post weekly schedules. It includes provisions for overtime pay and employee discipline.

source: An Act to extend the deadline for participation in the federal disaster relief program for counties affected by Hurricane Maria. SECTION 1. The deadline for affected counties in Puerto Rico to file for disaster relief is extended from January 31, 2024 to December 31, 2025. SECTION 2. Affected counties means those counties designated as disaster areas by FEMA in 2017. SECTION 3. This Act takes effect upon enactment.
reference: Extends the federal disaster relief filing deadline for Puerto Rico counties hit by Hurricane Maria from Jan 31, 2024 to Dec 31, 2025. Effective on enactment.
candidate: The deadline for Hurricane Maria disaster relief in Puerto Rico is extended to December 31, 2025. The Act covers counties FEMA designated as disaster areas in 2017 and takes effect upon enactment.

source: A Bill establishing a tax credit for taxpayers who install electric vehicle charging equipment at their primary residence. SECTION 1. Short title: Home EV Charging Tax Credit Act. SECTION 2. A taxpayer shall be allowed a credit equal to 30 percent of the cost of qualified residential charging equipment, up to 1000 dollars per residence. SECTION 3. The credit applies to equipment placed in service after December 31, 2024 and before January 1, 2030.
reference: Home EV Charging Tax Credit Act creates a 30% federal tax credit (capped at $1,000) for residential EV charger installation, for equipment in service Jan 1, 2025 through Dec 31, 2029.
candidate: Home EV Charging Tax Credit Act provides a 30 percent tax credit for residential electric vehicle charging equipment, capped at 1000 dollars per residence, for equipment placed in service after December 31, 2024 and before January 1, 2030.

source: A Bill to prohibit the use of solitary confinement in federal prisons for inmates with serious mental illness. SECTION 1. Short title: Mental Health Behind Bars Act. SECTION 2. The Bureau of Prisons shall not place an inmate with a documented serious mental illness in restrictive housing for more than 24 hours. SECTION 3. Documented serious mental illness includes schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and major depressive disorder. SECTION 4. The Bureau shall provide quarterly reports to Congress on compliance.
reference: Mental Health Behind Bars Act bars the federal Bureau of Prisons from placing inmates with serious mental illness (schizophrenia, bipolar, major depression) in solitary for more than 24 hours; requires quarterly compliance reports to Congress.
candidate: The new mental health legislation provides funding for prison health programs and improves access to care for incarcerated populations across the country.
